status: Run 2026-06-12 on Fluree v4.0.6. 850 WGPB graph-pattern queries against the full Wikidata all-dump (21.5B triples) on AWS r7a.8xlarge (32c/256GB). 100% completion: 0 timeouts, 0 errors. Geo 43 ms, median 33 ms; <100ms 73.8%, <1s 97.1%. 1 warmup + 3 runs, median (fractional-ms harness), 120s timeout.

benchmark.name: Wikidata Graph Pattern Benchmark (WGPB)
benchmark.description: 850 basic graph pattern queries: 17 abstract join shapes x 50 instances, predicates sampled from Wikidata. Each query is SELECT * ... LIMIT 1000 over prop/direct truthy edges.
benchmark.source: Hogan, Riveros, Rojas, Soto: 'A Worst-Case Optimal Join Algorithm for SPARQL' (ISWC 2019); query set Zenodo 4035223

families.J3/J4: object-object joins on a shared variable (3/4 triple patterns)
families.S2: bipartite square: two subjects sharing two objects
families.S4: converging square: two paths meeting at both ends
families.T2/T3/T4: subject out-stars: 2/3/4 patterns sharing a subject
families.TI2/TI3/TI4: object in-stars: 2/3/4 patterns sharing an object

dataset.name: Wikidata all-dump
dataset.description: Full Wikidata RDF dump (latest-all.nt.gz), all statements
dataset.version: snapshot dated 2026-06-04; pinned in S3 as wikidata-all/all-2026-06-04.nt.gz

engine.name: Fluree
engine.server: fluree server, HTTP SPARQL endpoint, cache budget 35% of RAM

methodology.notes: Index (794 GB) exceeds RAM (256 GB); the warmup run absorbs most cold-cache I/O. All 850 queries completed within the timeout.
